title: contract-tests
description: The verifier for protocol cells in the app family. A protocol (a boundary contract between collaborating capabilities) advances to `validated` only when every boundary it declares carries a contract test and the failure modes are covered. This rubric must itself be `validated` (deterministic + calibrated) before it gates any protocol. The gates are mechanical and run by bin/test-suite-check.py in contract mode against the protocol's declared boundaries + its contract-test result; consumer-fidelity and breaking-change discipline are the critic's judgment. The pristine reference is the protocol's own declared boundary set — supplied read-only, so the worker cannot quietly drop a boundary to make the gate pass.
maturity_precondition: This rubric cell must be validated (deterministic on fixed input; calibrated against the exemplar set) before it gates any protocol.

calibration.critic_model: an independent critic tier, NOT the code-authoring agent
recalibration_triggers: a boundary regression that the contract tests missed (a false-pass in the ledger)
a new boundary kind enters the protocol
calibration.note: Few-shot scored protocols spanning the scale: a strong protocol (every boundary has a contract test, failure modes covered), a happy-path-only protocol (no failure-mode tests), a boundary with no test at all, and a near-miss where a boundary was silently dropped from the declared set to dodge the coverage gate. An uncalibrated judge drifts generous toward protocols that test only the success path.

pristine_reference.enforced_by: the protocol's boundary set is supplied read-only (gate-verifier deny-on-write); validate.py mints the verdict from the EXTERNAL contract-test runner's exit status
pristine_reference.note: The set of boundaries the protocol must cover is the pristine reference: it is supplied read-only so the worker cannot drop a boundary to make 'every boundary has a test' trivially true. Coverage is scored against the declared set, not a set the worker can shrink. The pass/fail of each contract test is the external runner's exit status, not the worker's assertion.

dimensions.label: [gate]
dimensions.check: The protocol cell is well-formed (`protocol.{scope}.{slug}`, maturity excluded) and declares its boundaries: each boundary names what crosses it, in what form, under whose authority, and the failure modes.
dimensions.fail_meaning: An undeclared boundary cannot be contract-tested; the gate would pass vacuously.

dimensions.label: [gate]
dimensions.check: Every boundary in the protocol's declared (pristine) boundary set is exercised by >=1 contract test that runs green. Coverage is computed against the read-only declared set, so a worker cannot shrink the set to pass. This is a higher-order check: a completeness property over the boundary set, not a count of tests.
dimensions.fail_meaning: An untested boundary is an unverified contract; the integration breaks in production where no test looked.

dimensions.label: [gate]
dimensions.check: For each boundary, the declared failure modes (timeout, malformed input, authority denied, partial result) each have a contract test asserting the specified behaviour. Happy-path-only coverage fails here.
dimensions.fail_meaning: A boundary tested only on the success path is unverified exactly where production incidents come from.

dimensions.label: [review]
dimensions.check: Do the contract tests assert the contract the CONSUMER actually relies on (the observable behaviour at the boundary), or only the provider's internal shape? Would a change that breaks a real consumer turn a test red?
dimensions.fail_meaning: Provider-shaped contract tests pass while breaking the consumers the contract exists to protect.

dimensions.label: [review]
dimensions.check: Does the protocol version its boundaries and does a breaking change to a boundary force a visible, ledgered transition (not a silent edit)? Are backward-compatibility expectations stated and tested?
dimensions.fail_meaning: A boundary that can change silently turns every downstream consumer into an undetected breakage.

scoring.gates: ALL gate dimensions must pass; any gate failure returns the protocol to regenerating regardless of review score. bin/test-suite-check.py (contract mode) runs the gate dimensions mechanically and exits 0 iff all pass.
scoring.review: Weighted review score must clear each dimension threshold AND an aggregate of 80/100.
scoring.signal: On pass, the CRITIC (not the code-authoring agent) writes signals/{protocol-cell}/{ts}--contract-tests.json via the validation path. signals/ is deny-on-write to the worker (gate-signal). The gate verdict rests on the external contract-test runner's exit status and the read-only declared boundary set, not the worker's claim.
